the city's Infamous Q lines in this travel guide we'll walk through all the details you need to know when planning a trip to [Music] Florence you have a few different options if you're flying into Italy the most direct way is to fly to Florence's International Airport which is just 15 minutes from the city center from Florence's airport you can take a taxi to the downtown area for about €25 you can also fly into Pisa International Airport which is near pisa's train station with many 60-minute train departures to Florence a third option is to fly into Rome's fum macino airport once in Rome you can then take one of the many highspeed trains that connects these two cities in about 90 minutes this is the route we took to Florence so if you are stopping through Rome be sure to check out our helpful videos we made when we were there Florence is a very pedestrian friendly City and if you stay near the historical City Center you should be able to explore all the main sites on foot however if you're staying further outside the city there are also buses you can take you can purchase bus tickets from tobacco shops in Florence which you can easily identify by the large tea on their signs be sure to validate your tickets with the validation machines when you board the bus otherwise you could get stuck paying some very Hefty fines once validated your ticket is good for 90 minutes of travel buses run from 6:00 a.m. to 10: p.m. regularly but if you're traveling in the off hours you'll need to catch a not Tempo or night bus Which come less frequently so keep this in mind with Florence being one of Italy's most visited cities the time of year you visit will greatly shape your experience mid-may through September is considered to be Peak tourist season where the crowds and temperatures are typically the highest while busier the weather is usually nicer during the summer so that's a plus in August many Italians go on vacation themselves so some smaller restaurants and local shops may be closed but there'll still be plenty of spots open near the main attractions Florence's shoulder season extends from March through miday and then October through November since school is back in session this is often a more authentic Florentine experience as there are less crowds and more locals December through February is Florence's low season where there are minimal crowds and while the winter days are shorter Florence seldom gets snow so the temperatures will never get too frigid instead you should be prepared for some rain and fog if you plan to visit Florence in the [Music] winter given that Florence is a walkable City it's great to stay near the action for this reason many Travelers choose to stay in the central storico neighborhood which is Florence's historical district to determine how close your lodging is to the main Central Area check on Google Maps to see how close it is to the duo is this is the central main landmark in the area while expensive this is a great option if you can we stay in this small but efficient Airbnb in August for just under $80 a night and we were a 7-minute walk to the duo so it is possible to be in this area without breaking the bank if you're looking for an even greater deal consider staying in the ultranano district this is located across the pontaco bridge over the Arno River and is about a 20-minute walk to the duo this area is quieter cheaper and still have some great options to consider nowadays many attractions in Florence require tickets with some selling out weeks in advance to combat this it's recommended to purchase tickets for the hotspots online before your trip if you want to scope out some of the top things to do in Florence before your trip be sure to watch our video where we walk you through our favorite spots the hottest tickets in town tend to be for the Duo's Dome climb or gato's Bell Tower climb both these tickets get you into the ruins beneath the dwo the Baptist tree and the Opera gel dwo Museum so they're a good deal you'll need to pre-select what time of day you'll be hiking to the top though and given Florence can have scorching heat around midday in the summer consider a morning or evening climb to beat the heat another spot you'll need tickets for is the uzi Gallery which is one of Europe's premier art museums you can either pre-book tickets on their official site or if the tickets are sold out you can use third party sites like get your guide or Vier which may be slightly more expensive but may have more time slots available we'll link these below if you're interested if you want to see The Fame David statue you'll need tickets for the Academia Gallery which you can pre- purchase either from the official site or third party partner sites similar to the fuzi if you're traveling last minute and can't get any tickets we highly recommend the Galileo Museum which seldom sells out or has any lines at the ticket office inside you'll find antique scientific instruments from historic experiments and even Galileo's preserve finger we thought this was an incredibly interesting Museum that Blended history science and art and would highly recommend it and really quick we want to pause this video cuz we have an incoming announcement we started our own packing company and some of our products include small packing cubes and now officially whoa large packing cubes Corney designed these days of the week packing cubes to make organizing your life on the road so much easier never forget an outfit again with these days of the week packing cubes we sell them in our Amazon store which will link in the description box below so feel free to check them out if you're interested in buying any of these beautiful packing cubes let's jump back to the video unlike the US in most cases there's a fee if you need to use a public bathroom in Florence it costs between 50 cents and €1 to use a public bathroom so it's best to have a few coins handy for when you need them to find these just look for signs that say toilet or WC which stands for water closet there are several public water closets located around Florence's main areas to avoid this most large restaurants have bathrooms for paying customers and we even found that a 2-year-old cappuccino from Burger King saved us from paying for public toilets when we were elsewhere in Italy so consider this if you're trying to save some money and use the [Music] bathroom Italy was among the early adopters of the Euro so Euros are the currency you'll use during your visit if you prefer not to carry a hefty amount of cash you'll be glad to know that credit cards are widely accepted in most restaurants and attractions when it comes to tipping it's important to note that tipping is not a common practice in Italy it's not customary to add a tip to your restaurant bill or taxi fair but if you wish you can leave a Euro on the table as a gesture of appreciation for excellent service however it's worth mentioning that many restaurants in Florence may have a fixed cover charge per person for simply sitting at a table this charge known as kerto typically amounts to a Euro or two per person some restaurants don't impose this fee at all so feel free to ask before sitting down when it comes to Tasty Cuisine few countries can rival Italy as a cultural Hub of pasta and pizza Italy offers a wealth of incredible dishes in our Florence food tour video we explore Florence's iconic offerings and recommend some must try Foods so be sure to give that a watch as well when ensure you have comfortable and sturdy Footwear that can handle sometimes uneven cobblestone streets and don't forget a reusable water bottle which you can conveniently fill from public fountains along the way considering the potentially High Cost of sunscreen and tourist areas it's a good idea to bring your own to to save money we carried a dry sunscreen stick that worked super well for us and was very travel friendly also consider a small coin purse as 1 to2 euro coins are commonly given as change another thing to bring is a day pack that can protect your valuables from Italy's cover your knees we'll link below our favorite versions of all these [Music] items like any major city sadly there are always people out to make a quick Buck at tourist expense one common scam in Florence is the friendship bracelet scam here scammers approach tourists especially near popular attractions like the fuzi gallery or the Ponto Veo offering to tie friendship bracelets on your wrist
